step_rationale: Monocyte/macrophage recruitment and adhesion to atherosclerotic plaque and endothelium.
mechanism: CCR2 mediates monocyte chemotaxis and adhesion into the atherosclerotic plaque, driving plaque inflammation and destabilization.

references.title: Genetic Variation in the Chemokine Network and Atherosclerosis Risk.
Spatial multiomics of acute myocardial infarction reveals immune cell infiltration through the endocardium.
Influence of LDL cholesterol and Lp(a) on monocytes and macrophages in atherosclerosis.
C-reactive protein, cytokines and inflammation in cardiovascular diseases.

trials.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind Placebo- Controlled Phase 2a Study to Assess the Effect of CCR2 Antagonism by MLN1202 on Atherosclerotic Inflammation in Subjects With Stable Atherosclerotic Cardiovascular Disease Using FDG PET/CT Imaging
